Name: information_theoretic_model_compression
Title: Exploring the Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of neural network compression can be characterized using rate-distortion theory, and that this framework can be used to derive novel compression algorithms that achieve better tradeoffs between compression ratio and model performance.
Related Work: Recent works have applied information-theoretic principles to neural network compression, including [1], [2], and [3]. However, these works have focused on specific aspects of compression, such as pruning or quantization, and have not fully explored the information-theoretic limits of compression. Our proposal aims to fill this gap by providing a comprehensive framework for understanding the fundamental limits of neural network compression.
Abstract: Neural network compression is a crucial technique for reducing the computational and memory requirements of deep learning models. However, the fundamental limits of compression are not well understood. In this proposal, we aim to explore the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression using rate-distortion theory. We will derive novel compression algorithms that achieve better tradeoffs between compression ratio and model performance, and evaluate their performance on a range of benchmarks. Our work has the potential to significantly improve the efficiency of deep learning models, and to enable their deployment on resource-constrained devices.
Experiments: Derive a rate-distortion function for neural networks, characterizing the fundamental limits of compression
Develop a novel compression algorithm based on rate-distortion theory, and evaluate its performance on a range of benchmarks
Compare the performance of our algorithm with existing compression techniques, such as pruning and quantization
Evaluate the tradeoffs between compression ratio and model performance, and explore the limits of compression for different types of neural networks
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of neural networks may make it difficult to derive a tractable rate-distortion function
The performance of our compression algorithm may be sensitive to the choice of hyperparameters and compression ratio
The evaluation of our algorithm may be limited by the availability of suitable benchmarks and evaluation metrics
